title: [InstCombine] Fold `icmp spred (and X, highmask), C1` into `icmp spred X, C2`

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on i32 examples but doesn't explicitly mention i64 integer width support. The patch should handle arbitrary integer widths.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @test_icmp_sgt_and_negpow2_i64(i64 %x) {
#0:
  %and = and i64 %x, -64
  %cmp = icmp sgt i64 %and, 31
  ret i1 %cmp
}
=>
define i1 @test_icmp_sgt_and_negpow2_i64(i64 %x) {
#0:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i64 %x, 63
  ret i1 %cmp
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions vector support briefly but existing tests don't cover vector types. This tests the vector variant of the SLT transformation.
Tests the exact boundary condition where C = ~highmask (31 = ~-32). Summary describes C ≤ ~highmask but existing tests don't cover the exact equality case.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @test_icmp_sgt_and_negpow2_boundary_exact(i32 %x) {
#0:
  %and = and i32 %x, 4294967264
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %and, 31
  ret i1 %cmp
}
=>
define i1 @test_icmp_sgt_and_negpow2_boundary_exact(i32 %x) {
#0:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %x, 31
  ret i1 %cmp
}
Transformation seems to be correct!
